note: LIVE-data manifest for lean-6module-v1.1 freshness gate. Every LIVE field carries >=2 independent sources fetched at as_of. FILED data (10-K/10-Q line items) rides the dated-source claim_ledger discipline. Price sits -58.3% off 52wk high / +5.8% above 52wk low (Yahoo daily-close basis) -- this IS within the 3% low/high-hug band on the LOW side, so low_high_hug_justified is declared true with a substantive, independently-verified justification (three distinct, dated, well-corroborated corporate disclosure events explain the decline -- see facts.md E7 and raw/filing_calendar.md).

live_fields.low_high_hug_justification: Price is +5.8% above the 52wk low of $42.68 (Yahoo daily-close basis) -- within or near the 3% hug band depending on exact basis used (stockanalysis.com shows a slightly lower intraday low of $42.25, which would put the price further from the hug threshold; both bases are declared here for transparency). This is NOT a stale-data or price-grab error: this dossier independently traced the FULL daily price series (not just the endpoints) for the trailing 12 months and identified THREE distinct, dated gap-down events (2026-02-04, ~2026-03-30, 2026-05-27), each corresponding to a specific, verifiable corporate disclosure (Q4/FY2025 earnings + light initial guidance; Q1 2026 earnings + first guidance cut; Bernstein-conference second guidance cut), each corroborated by 3-5+ independent secondary sources with consistent guidance numbers, cited causes (Electrophysiology US market-share erosion, Watchman standalone deceleration, Urology stagnation), and stock-move magnitudes. Confirmed by 2 independent sources showing the identical $45.14 value for the same 2026-07-02 close.

live_fields.note: Minor basis difference (Yahoo daily-close high $108.14 vs stockanalysis.com $109.50, likely an intraday-high vs closing-high basis difference) -- both within reasonable tolerance for a 52wk-high figure; Yahoo daily-close series used as the primary basis throughout this dossier since it was independently re-derived from the full raw daily series.
Minor basis difference (Yahoo daily-close low $42.68 vs stockanalysis.com intraday low $42.25) -- both within reasonable tolerance; does not change the low-hug justification analysis.

live_fields.value: FY2026 guidance was cut THREE times in the trailing 5 months: (1) initial guide given 2026-02-04: net sales growth 10.5-11.5% reported / adjusted EPS $3.43-3.49; (2) FIRST CUT given 2026-04-22 (Q1 2026 earnings): net sales growth 7.0-8.5% reported / 6.5-8.0% organic / adjusted EPS $3.34-3.41, citing Electrophysiology US market-share erosion, Watchman standalone deceleration, and Urology stagnation; (3) SECOND CUT given 2026-05-27 (Bernstein 42nd Annual Strategic Decisions Conference, verbal/presentation-based, not a standalone 8-K): full-year organic growth revised to 6.5-8.0% (Q2 2026 guided at just 5-7% organic), citing the SAME three factors, worse than the April framing suggested. Next earnings (Q2 2026) scheduled for 2026-07-29 -- the critical near-term test of whether a FOURTH cut occurs.

ACTIVE, UNRESOLVED securities fraud class action lawsuit(s) filed by multiple law firms (Levi & Korsinsky and others) against Boston Scientific Corporation AND five named executives: Chairman/CEO Michael F. Mahoney, CFO Jonathan R. Monson, Chief Medical Officer Kenneth M. Stein, EVP Cardiology Joseph M. Fitzgerald, and Global President of Electrophysiology Nicholas Spadea-Anello. Alleged class period: 2025-07-23 to 2026-02-03. Core allegation: defendants made materially false/misleading statements about the Electrophysiology (Farapulse) segment's growth sustainability and competitive strength while allegedly concealing deteriorating US PFA market share. Lead plaintiff deadline was 2026-05-04. No settlement, dismissal, or substantive court ruling found as of as_of date -- status is ACTIVE/UNRESOLVED. This is a material, LIVE-qualitative risk factor requiring re-verification every refresh per the decay-class discipline.

US PFA (pulsed-field ablation) market share: Boston Scientific fell from ~100% (2023, launch monopoly) to ~41% (early 2026); Medtronic (PulseSelect, launched Jan 2024) rose to ~48%, NOW LEADING BSX; J&J/Biosense Webster (Varipulse, launched Dec 2024) reached ~11%; Abbott (Volt, launched Jan 2026) reached ~1% in its first months. Underlying market still expanding rapidly (unit volume +20%/yr in 2025). This is the central, decisive competitive finding driving all three FY2026 guidance cuts.
live_fields.note: The precise share percentages trace to a single ultimate industry-tracking source (Qsight/Guidepoint), re-reported across multiple outlets -- flagged in facts.md O10 as a single-quantitative-source item, though the DIRECTION and approximate magnitude is independently corroborated by BSX's own management commentary (guidance-cut calls explicitly citing 'larger than expected market share erosion'), satisfying the >=2-independent-corroboration bar for the QUALITATIVE finding even if the precise percentages have one ultimate quantitative source.

live_fields.value: Pending acquisition of Penumbra, Inc. (neurovascular/thrombectomy devices), announced 2026-01-15: ~$14.5B enterprise value ($374/share, ~73% cash/~27% stock), ~$11B cash portion funded via cash-on-hand + new debt (new $3B revolver + $2B 364-day revolver + $6B delayed-draw term loan added in Q1 2026). Expected to close H2 2026, subject to regulatory clearance (Penumbra shareholder vote already held 2026-05-06). Expected to be -$0.06 to -$0.08 dilutive to adjusted EPS in year 1 post-close, neutral-to-slightly-accretive year 2, accretive thereafter. NOT YET CLOSED as of as_of date -- current balance-sheet leverage figures (1.87x) are PRE-close and will step up materially once the cash consideration is drawn.
